query: CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi başvuru karar - latest status as of late June / early July 2026. Has Özgür Özel's team filed the application under Civil Code Art 75 requesting appointment of a 3-person çağrı heyeti to convene a CHP extraordinary congress? Has any court issued a decision?
answer:
## Summary

**Has Özel's team filed the Art. 75 çağrı heyeti application? → Not confirmed filed as of July 11, 2026.**
**Has any court issued a decision on a çağrı heyeti? → No.**

Key findings:

1. **Announced but not confirmed filed.** After the 21 May 2026 "mutlak butlan" (absolute nullity) ruling removed Özel and returned CHP leadership to Kılıçdaroğlu, Özel's side collected notarized delegate signatures (833 claimed; 820 counted valid by headquarters) demanding an extraordinary congress. When the headquarters declined to call it, numerous outlets reported around **29 June 2026** that Özel's team "would apply today" to the Ankara Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi for a **3-person çağrı heyeti under Civil Code Art. 75**.

2. **But the filing was walked back / delayed.** Özel's own legal advisor **Gül Çiftçi told Odatv** the application would NOT be filed yet, citing unmet "technical matters." As of **11 July 2026** (Türkiye Gazetesi/Haber7), the petition still had not been filed — the team said it would file "next week." Reports say the court could rule within **1–3 months** of filing, and if granted, a congress would follow within **45 days**.

3. **No çağrı heyeti decision exists.** The only related court actions went **against** Özel: the İstinaf rejected a clarification request on **26 June 2026** and had rejected injunction objections on **22 May 2026**.

4. **Context:** Momentum was shifting toward founding a **new party** (MP Süleyman Bülbül floated ~100 MPs after 21 July 2026), with the Yargıtay's final "mutlak butlan" ruling pending and **21 July 2026** cited as pivotal.

Note: I encountered some tension between "application today" headlines (June 29) and the primary-source statements from Özel's legal team indicating no filing — I weighted the direct-quote sources (Çiftçi/Odatv) and the most recent July 11 reporting as most reliable for status.

sources.title: CHP'de gündem kurultay için 'çağrı heyeti'... Başvuru bugün denilmişti ama... Gül Çiftçi Odatv'ye anlattı
sources.snippet: Özel'in hukukçu kurmayı Gül Çiftçi: 'Kurultay için teknik bazı hususlar oluşuyor. O aşamaya gelinmediği için henüz bir başvuru yapmayacağız.'

sources.title: Özgür Özel için seçenekler tükeniyor! CHP'den kopacak ekip için zaman daralıyor
sources.snippet: July 11, 2026: deadline for headquarters expires; application not yet filed, planned for next week; Ankara Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi could decide within 1–3 months.

sources.title: Özgür Özel için yolun sonu! CHP'yle ipler kopuyor
sources.snippet: As of July 11, 2026 the çağrı heyeti application had not been filed; team plans to file next week; court could rule within 1–3 months.

sources.title: CHP'de Tarihi Bölünme Resti: '21 Temmuz'da 100 Milletvekiliyle Yeni Oluşum Başlatabiliriz'
sources.snippet: MP Süleyman Bülbül: if Yargıtay rules against Özel, could start a new political formation with ~100 MPs after 21 July 2026.

key_facts: The çağrı heyeti application under Turkish Civil Code Art. 75 (three-person convening committee) was announced/prepared by Özgür Özel's team but NOT confirmed formally filed as of July 11, 2026.
No Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Peace Court) decision appointing or rejecting a çağrı heyeti had been issued as of the latest reporting (July 11, 2026).

Özel's legal advisor Gül Çiftçi told Odatv the application would not be filed yet because 'technical matters' for the congress were not ready — contradicting 'application today' headlines around June 29.
As of July 11, 2026 (Türkiye Gazetesi/Haber7), Özel's team said it intended to file the court petition 'next week'; the court could rule within 1–3 months of filing.

The crisis stems from the Ankara BAM 36th Civil Chamber's 'mutlak butlan' (absolute nullity) decision of 21 May 2026, which removed Özel and returned CHP leadership provisionally to Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu.

Özel's side submitted notarized delegate signatures (reported 833; administration counted 820 valid) to headquarters demanding an extraordinary congress; a bylaw deadline (~26 June, Friday) passed without the congress being called.
If the çağrı heyeti request is accepted, CHP would reportedly hold an extraordinary congress within at most 45 days.
Related Özel-camp applications were rejected: on 26 June 2026 the İstinaf rejected a clarification ('karar açıklanması') request; on 22 May 2026 it rejected objections to the injunction.
Momentum within Özel's faction was shifting toward founding a new party; MP Süleyman Bülbül said ~100 MPs could launch a new formation after 21 July 2026 if Yargıtay rules against Özel.
The Yargıtay's final ruling on the 'mutlak butlan' case was pending, with 21 July 2026 cited as a pivotal date.

data_points.metric: Notarized delegate signatures submitted for extraordinary congress
Valid signatures per CHP headquarters after disciplinary referrals/withdrawals
Members of requested çağrı heyeti (convening committee) under Civil Code Art. 75
Maximum time to extraordinary congress if çağrı heyeti request accepted
Estimated time for Ankara Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi to rule after filing
MPs cited for potential new political formation after 21 July 2026
